questions.text: What is the significance of the title 'As-Sajdah'?
questions.answer: It is named after Verse 15, which describes the true believers who fall down in prostration when reminded of Allah's verses.

questions.text: What is the response to the claim that the Prophet (SAW) invented the Quran?
questions.answer: It is the truth from your Lord to warn a people to whom no warner has come before.

questions.text: In how many days did Allah create the heavens and the earth?
questions.answer: Six days.

questions.text: How does Allah describe the 'length of a day' in Verse 5?
questions.answer: A thousand years of those which you count.

questions.text: What did Allah breathe into the human after fashioning him?
questions.answer: He breathed into him from His [created] soul (Ruh).

questions.text: What three faculties are specifically mentioned as being given by Allah in Verse 9?
questions.answer: Hearing, sight, and hearts (intellect).

questions.text: What will the Angel of Death do (Verse 11)?
questions.answer: He will take your souls, and then to your Lord you will be returned.

questions.text: How will the criminals look before their Lord on Judgment Day?
questions.answer: With their heads lowered, saying 'Our Lord, we have seen and heard, so return us [to the world]; we will do righteousness'.

questions.text: What is the characteristic of believers regarding their beds (Verse 16)?
questions.answer: They forsake their beds (arise for night prayer), calling upon their Lord in fear and hope.

questions.text: What does Verse 17 say about the reward hidden for the righteous?
questions.answer: No soul knows what has been hidden for them of comfort for eyes (joy) as reward for what they used to do.

questions.text: Are the believer and the defiantly disobedient (Fasiq) equal?
questions.answer: No, they are not equal.

questions.text: Where will the home of the 'Fasiqun' (defiantly disobedient) be?
questions.answer: The Fire; every time they wish to get out, they are returned to it.

questions.text: What is the 'lesser punishment' mentioned in Verse 21?
questions.answer: The trials and calamities of this world, which come before the greater punishment of the Hereafter, so that people may return to the right path.

questions.text: Who is more unjust than the one who is reminded of the verses of his Lord and then turns away?
questions.answer: No one; Allah will surely take retribution from the criminals.

questions.text: Which Prophet was given the Scripture before Muhammad (SAW) as mentioned here?
questions.answer: Musa (AS).

questions.text: What was the role of the leaders (Imams) among the Children of Israel?
questions.answer: They guided by Allah's command as long as they were patient and certain of His signs.

questions.text: What is the 'Day of Decision' (Yawm al-Fath) mentioned at the end of the Surah?
questions.answer: The Day of Judgment; on that day, the faith of those who disbelieved will not benefit them.

questions.text: How does Allah describe the earth's vegetation in Verse 27?
questions.answer: He drives water to barren land and brings forth crops from which their livestock and they themselves eat.

questions.text: What should the Prophet do regarding those who wait for his downfall (Verse 30)?
questions.answer: Turn away from them and wait; indeed, they are also waiting.

questions.text: How many verses of 'Sajdah' (obligatory prostration) are in this Surah?
questions.answer: One (Verse 15).
